title: inZORi Active Space: N₂ Dissociation — Bio-Adaptive Selection of CASSCF Active Spaces (6-31G, 3 geometries)
summary: ZOR discovers the optimal CASSCF(6,6) active space for N₂ dissociation across 3 geometries (R=1.1, 1.5, 2.0 Å) using 6-31G basis, evaluating only 2.9% of the 18,564 possible active spaces. The bio-adaptive population finds 10 degenerate optimal spaces (symmetry-equivalent under D∞h) with identical energies, demonstrating that ZOR naturally discovers orbital degeneracy without any chemical priors.
verdict: ZOR FINDS GLOBAL OPTIMUM EVALUATING ONLY 2.9% OF SEARCH SPACE — 10 DEGENERATE OPTIMA DISCOVERED
